questionText: A Ferris wheel car's height is $h = 12 - 5\cos(6t)^\circ - 12\sin(6t)^\circ$ metres, where $t$ is in seconds. Find the minimum height and the values of $t$ in $0 \le t < 60$ for which $h \le 6$.

steps.explanation: Factoring out the minus lets us combine the bracket into one sine.
Substituting gives a single-sine model with a leading minus.
The height is least when the sine is at its maximum of $1$; here the model gives $-1$, so in practice the wheel would be offset, but mathematically the minimum of the expression is $-1$.
We want the times when the height is at most $6$ m.
Subtracting $12$, dividing by $-13$ (which flips the inequality) isolates the sine.
The sine equals $0.4615$ at these angles; it is above between them.

steps.description: Form the inequality band for the argument
steps.explanation: Within the first cycle the sine is at or above $0.4615$ across this band.
Subtracting the phase and dividing by $6$ gives the first time window (in seconds) when the height is at most $6$ m.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: Minimum height $-1$ m; $h \le 6$ for approximately $0.81 \le t \le 21.6$ s (repeating each $60$ s).
